   "source": [
    "# Non-Gaussian Noise Analysis: Rayleigh and Gaussian-Chi\n",
    "\n",
    "This tutorial demonstrates the implementation of a comprehensive non-Gaussian noise analysis toolkit in GWexpy, based on research by Yamamura (2024) and Yamamoto (2015-2016).\n",
    "\n",
    "### Contents\n",
    "1. **Noise Simulation**: Generating non-stationary and scattered light noise.\n",
    "2. **Rayleigh Statistics**: Testing deviations from Rayleigh distribution.\n",
    "3. **GauCh (Modified KS Test)**: Advanced non-Gaussianity detection.\n",
    "4. **Student-t Indicator**: Measuring distribution tail thickness.\n",
    "5. **Data Quality Flags**: Generating veto segments automatically.\n",
    "6. **Evaluation & Visualization**: ROC curves and composite dashboards."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "fff52b96",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-23T04:01:19.286074Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-23T04:01:19.285804Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.559203Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.558633Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 8.286429,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.562131+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:01:19.275702+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.noise import scatter_light_noise, transient_gaussian_noise\n",
    "from gwexpy.plot.gauch_dashboard import plot_gauch_dashboard\n",
    "from gwexpy.statistics import to_segments\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5c5c9f46",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.017618,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.597610+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.579992+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Noise Simulation\n",
    "We simulate two types of non-Gaussian noise models used in KAGRA characterization.\n",
    "- **Model I**: Transient Gaussian noise (glitches).\n",
    "- **Model II**: Scattered light noise (stationary non-Gaussianity)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "210d84a8",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.655355Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.654908Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.169303Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.166862Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 43.546054,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.177774+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:01:27.631720+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 1200x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 3,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 1200x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "duration = 32.0\n",
    "fs = 1024.0\n",
    "\n",
    "# Model I: Glitch injection\n",
    "ts_glitch = transient_gaussian_noise(duration, fs, A1=20.0, name='Glitchy Data')\n",
    "\n",
    "# Model II: Scattered light\n",
    "ts_scatter = scatter_light_noise(duration, fs, A2=1e-12, name='Scattered Light')\n",
    "\n",
    "ts_glitch.plot()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b1ef161a",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.011035,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.203249+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.192214+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Rayleigh Spectrogram\n",
    "The Rayleigh statistic $R$ measures the consistency of the ASD distribution with a Rayleigh distribution. We use the `rayleigh_test` method to get a p-value map."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "72142fa5",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.250285Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.250047Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.182371Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.181584Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 24.946926,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.185667+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:11.238741+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<matplotlib.colorbar.Colorbar at 0x7f3479a176d0>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 4,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 900x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "rs_p = ts_glitch.rayleigh_test(fftlength=0.4, stride=0.5, overlap=0.2, n_samples=20)\n",
    "rs_p.plot(yscale='log', title='Rayleigh Test p-value Map')\n",
    "plt.colorbar(next((c for c in plt.gca().get_children() if hasattr(c, 'get_clim')), None), label='p-value')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8ca95797",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.070773,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.272580+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.201807+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. GauCh (Modified KS Test)\n",
    "GauCh is a more sensitive test for non-Gaussianity using a modified Kolmogorov-Smirnov test."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "c9546d8f",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.330430Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.330172Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-23T04:02:57.707405Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-23T04:02:57.704322Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 21.391429,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:57.708038+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:36.316609+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<matplotlib.colorbar.Colorbar at 0x7f347b0dd410>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 5,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 900x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "gauch_res = ts_glitch.gauch(fftlength=1.0, window=8)\n",
    "gauch_res.pvalue_map.plot(yscale='log', title='GauCh p-value Map')\n",
    "plt.colorbar(next((c for c in plt.gca().get_children() if hasattr(c, 'get_clim')), None), label='p-value')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "76781817",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.012976,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:57.734413+00:00",
     "exception": false,

     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 4. Student-t Indicator\n",
    "The Student-t indicator fits a Student-t distribution to FFT components and outputs the degree of freedom $\\nu$."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "22f707a4",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-23T04:02:57.792173Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-23T04:02:57.791940Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-23T04:10:07.791935Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-23T04:10:07.791455Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 430.018731,
     "end_time": "2026-04-23T04:10:07.795533+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-23T04:02:57.776802+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "<matplotlib.colorbar.Colorbar at 0x7f347ae74c90>"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 6,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 900x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "nu_spec = ts_glitch.student_t_spectrogram(fftlength=1.0, window=8, frange=(10, 200))\n",
    "nu_spec.plot(yscale='log', title='Student-t nu Map')\n",
    "plt.colorbar(next((c for c in plt.gca().get_children() if hasattr(c, 'get_clim')), None), label='nu')"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## 5. Data Quality Flags\n",
    "We can automatically generate veto segments where the p-value is below a threshold."
   ]
  },

   "source": [

    "print(dq_flag)\n",
    "dq_flag.plot()"
   ]
  },
